gene_description: Interleukin-6 (IL6) — pro-inflammatory cytokine mediating exercise-induced inflammatory response and muscle repair and remodeling

source.title: A functional polymorphism in the IL6 gene promoter (-174G/C) regulates gene expression and associates with disease risk in adults
source.finding: rs1800795 -174G allele associated with higher IL-6 expression and enhanced post-exercise IL-6 response. G carriers show less exercise-induced muscle damage (lower CK) and faster recovery vs CC carriers.

CC.effect: Homozygous for C allele (-174C/-174C). Low baseline IL-6 expression but exaggerated post-exercise IL-6 response. Higher circulating IL-6 after eccentric exercise. Increased muscle soreness and higher levels of creatine kinase (muscle damage marker). Prolonged recovery time following intense or damaging exercise.
CC.recommendation: Emphasize recovery strategies: adequate protein (1.6-2.2 g/kg/day), carbohydrate around training, omega-3 supplementation, anti-inflammatory foods (berries, leafy greens, fatty fish). Prioritize sleep (7-9 hours). Consider active recovery and stretching. Allow longer recovery between intense sessions. Cold water immersion may help reduce soreness.

GC.effect: Heterozygous for G/C alleles. Intermediate IL-6 production and post-exercise response. Moderate exercise-induced muscle inflammation and recovery time.
GC.recommendation: Standard training recovery practices. Adequate nutrition, sleep, and progressive training progression. Monitor recovery between intense sessions.

CG.effect: Heterozygous for G/C alleles. Intermediate IL-6 production and post-exercise response. Moderate exercise-induced muscle inflammation and recovery time.
CG.recommendation: Standard training recovery practices. Adequate nutrition, sleep, and progressive training progression. Monitor recovery between intense sessions.

GG.effect: Homozygous for G allele (-174G/-174G). Higher baseline and post-exercise IL-6 expression. Optimal IL-6 mediated muscle repair response. Lower exercise-induced muscle damage markers (CK). Faster recovery and less DOMS (delayed-onset muscle soreness) following eccentric exercise.
GG.recommendation: Favorable recovery profile allows more frequent intense training. Standard training nutrition and recovery practices sufficient. Can tolerate higher training frequency and volume.
